secondary_archetype: Technical Program Manager / People Manager (Director-level PM)

level_signals: 8+ years of product management experience, including experience leading or mentoring Product Managers
Build, lead, and mentor a team of Product Managers
Comp range $236K-$287K base, consistent with Director-level at a mid-size DTC brand

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience, including experience leading or mentoring Product Managers
Proven experience owning product strategy across e-commerce, marketing technology, retail, or omni channel platforms
Strong track record of delivering customer-facing and operational products that drive measurable business impact
Experience managing and scaling cross-functional teams across engineering, UX/design, and QA
Deep familiarity with e-commerce platforms, CMS, CRM, marketing automation tools, and analytics platforms (e.g., Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, or similar)
Strong understanding of customer journeys, conversion funnels, omnichannel experiences, and digital marketing ecosystems
Experience establishing product management best practices, including roadmapping, prioritization, and discovery frameworks
Expertise in writing and overseeing product requirements, user stories, and backlog management in tools like Jira, Asana, or similar
Comfort operating in Agile or Scrum environments at scale
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and translate insights into strategic action
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field (business, technology, or similar)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: MBA or equivalent experience is a plus

scope.surface: Ecommerce/Marketing, Retail, and Wholesale digital and omnichannel product domains
scope.business_model: DTC e-commerce + retail (omnichannel), sustainable fashion brand
primary_metrics_inferred: Roadmap delivery and product team velocity
core_responsibilities: Define and own the product vision, strategy, and multi-year roadmap across Ecommerce/Marketing, Retail, and Wholesale domains
Build, lead, and mentor a team of Product Managers
Serve as the central leadership hub between Engineering, CX, Marketing, Retail, Wholesale, and executive stakeholders
Oversee multiple cross-functional squads embedded within business groups
Guide translation of business needs and customer insights into product requirements, user stories, and success metrics
Establish prioritization frameworks aligned to business value, customer impact, and technical feasibility
Drive end-to-end product delivery from discovery through launch, optimization, and iteration
Leverage data, analytics, and experimentation frameworks to inform decision-making
Scale product management processes, tools, and operating models

compensation.equity_form: additional discretionary bonuses/incentives mentioned but no equity explicitly stated

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($236K-$287K base)
Clear Director-level scope and title explicitly stated in JD body
Well-defined cross-functional ownership across Ecommerce, Retail, and Wholesale
Mentions people management responsibilities explicitly — avoids vague 'lead' title inflation
Sustainability-forward brand with clear product mission
red_flags: Onsite San Francisco only — no remote or hybrid option stated, limiting candidate pool
Role title says 'Front End' but JD body focuses on omnichannel/e-commerce strategy; the 'Front End' framing may be misleading or narrower than described
Equity not mentioned despite Director-level comp range — unusual omission at this seniority
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as onsite — no hybrid or remote language in JD body)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product domains)
seniority.level (inferred from 'Director level' label in JD body and comp range)
ambiguities: Role title is 'Product Manager Lead, Front End' but the JD body never mentions 'front end' engineering or UI-layer specificity — responsibilities cover full omnichannel stack including Retail and Wholesale; 'Front End' in the title may refer to customer-facing surfaces rather than frontend engineering
Compensation note mentions 'additional discretionary bonuses/incentives' but no equity (RSUs/options) is explicitly offered — unclear whether equity is part of the package at this level
